# CLI Reference

> Auto-generated by mtix v0.1.0-beta-1-g12189ab. Do not edit manually.

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED: CLI_COMMANDS -->
## mtix

**Usage:** `mtix`

AI-native micro issue manager for code-generating LLMs

mtix (micro-tix) is a hierarchical task management system designed
for safety-critical environments where LLM coding agents operate.

It provides micro issue tracking with infinite-depth decomposition,
prompt chain propagation, and multi-agent orchestration.

### Subcommands

- `agent` — Manage agent lifecycle
- `annotate <id> <text>` — Add an annotation to a node
- `backup <path>` — Create a backup of the mtix database
- `blocked` — List nodes blocked by dependencies
- `cancel <id>` — Cancel a node with mandatory reason
- `claim <id>` — Claim a node for an agent
- `comment <id> <text>` — Add a comment annotation to a node
- `config` — Manage mtix configuration
- `context <id>` — Show assembled context chain for a node
- `create <title>` — Create a new node
- `decompose <parent-id> [title1 title2...]` — Create multiple children under a node atomically
- `defer <id>` — Defer a node until a specified time
- `delete <id>` — Soft-delete a node
- `dep` — Manage node dependencies
- `docs` — Manage agent-facing documentation
- `done <id>` — Mark a node as done
- `export` — Export nodes to JSON
- `gc` — Run garbage collection (clean expired soft-deletes)
- `import <file>` — Import nodes from JSON export
- `init` — Initialize a new mtix project
- `list` — List nodes with filters
- `mcp` — Run as MCP server over stdio (for LLM agent integration)
- `micro <title>` — Create a micro task (requires --under)
- `migrate` — Run database migrations
- `orphans` — List root-level nodes (no parent)
- `plugin` — Install mtix as a plugin for AI coding agents
- `progress <id>` — Show progress for a node and its subtree
- `prompt <id> <text>` — Set or update a node's prompt text
- `ready` — List nodes ready for work (unblocked, unassigned)
- `reopen <id>` — Reopen a closed node
- `rerun <id>` — Invalidate and reprocess descendants of a node
- `resolve-annotation <node-id> <annotation-id>` — Resolve an annotation on a node
- `restore <id>` — Restore an invalidated node to its previous status
- `search` — Search nodes with advanced filters
- `serve` — Start the mtix HTTP/WebSocket/gRPC server
- `session` — Manage agent sessions
- `show <id>` — Show full details of a node
- `stale` — List nodes with stale agent assignments
- `stats` — Show project statistics
- `sync` — Check or fix sync between SQLite and tasks.json
- `tree <id>` — Show ASCII tree visualization
- `unclaim <id>` — Release a node assignment
- `undelete <id>` — Restore a soft-deleted node
- `update <id>` — Update a node's fields
- `verify [id]` — Verify content hash integrity
---

## create

**Usage:** `create <title>`

Create a new node

Create a new node. Use --under to create a child node.

### Flags

| Flag | Short | Description | Default |
|------|-------|-------------|---------|
| `--acceptance` |  | Acceptance criteria |  |
| `--assign` |  | Assign to agent/user |  |
| `--description` |  | Node description |  |
| `--labels` |  | Comma-separated labels |  |
| `--priority` |  | Priority (1=critical, 5=backlog) | 3 |
| `--prompt` |  | Node prompt (FR-12.5) |  |
| `--type` |  | Node type (bug, feature, task, chore) |  |
| `--under` |  | Parent node ID |  |
---

## decompose

**Usage:** `decompose <parent-id> [title1 title2...]`

Create multiple children under a node atomically

Create multiple child nodes under a parent in a single atomic operation.
All children are created together or none at all.

Two modes:
  Positional args:  mtix decompose PROJ-1 "Task A" "Task B"
  JSONL file:       mtix decompose PROJ-1 --file plan.jsonl
  JSONL stdin:      mtix decompose PROJ-1 --file -

Use --dry-run to preview proposed nodes without creating them:
  mtix decompose PROJ-1 --file plan.jsonl --dry-run

JSONL format (one JSON object per line):
  {"title":"Add login endpoint","prompt":"Implement POST /auth/login..."}
  {"title":"Add token refresh","prompt":"...","priority":1,"labels":["auth"]}

JSONL is preferred for LLM plan output:
  - Each line is independently valid (partial output is usable)
  - No bracket matching or trailing comma issues
  - Streaming-friendly and append-friendly

### Flags

| Flag | Short | Description | Default |
|------|-------|-------------|---------|
| `--dry-run` | `-n` | Preview proposed nodes without creating them | false |
| `--file` | `-f` | JSONL plan file (use - for stdin) |  |
---

## docs

**Usage:** `docs`

Manage agent-facing documentation

Generate and manage agent-facing documentation files.

Produces markdown files in the project's .mtix/docs/ directory that LLM agents
read to understand how to use mtix. Generated from runtime introspection
of CLI commands, state machine, MCP tools, and configuration.

### Subcommands

- `generate` — Generate agent-facing documentation
---

## generate

**Usage:** `generate`

Generate agent-facing documentation

Generate all 11 documentation files in the project's .mtix/docs/ directory.

Auto-generated files (CLI_REFERENCE.md, STATUS_MACHINE.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md)
are always regenerated. Template-based files are only created if missing,
unless --force is used.

### Flags

| Flag | Short | Description | Default |
|------|-------|-------------|---------|
| `--force` |  | Force regeneration of all files including template-based ones | false |
---

## init

**Usage:** `init`

Initialize a new mtix project

Initialize a new mtix project in the current directory.

Creates .mtix/ directory structure with config, database, and logs.
Generates agent documentation in .mtix/docs/.

### Flags

| Flag | Short | Description | Default |
|------|-------|-------------|---------|
| `--prefix` |  | Project prefix for node IDs (uppercase, max 20 chars) | PROJ |
---

## mcp

**Usage:** `mcp`

Run as MCP server over stdio (for LLM agent integration)

Starts mtix as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server using
stdio transport. JSON-RPC 2.0 messages are read from stdin and
responses are written to stdout.

This command is designed to be invoked by MCP clients such as
Claude Desktop, Cursor, Claude Code, or any MCP-compatible agent.

Use --project to specify the project directory. This allows a single
mtix binary to serve multiple projects without relying on cwd:

  mtix mcp --project /path/to/project-a
  mtix mcp --project /path/to/project-b

All logs are redirected to .mtix/logs/mtix.log to prevent
contamination of the protocol stream.

Example MCP client configuration:

  {
    "mcpServers": {
      "mtix": {
        "command": "mtix",
        "args": ["mcp", "--project", "/path/to/your/project"]
      }
    }
  }

### Flags

| Flag | Short | Description | Default |
|------|-------|-------------|---------|
| `--project` | `-C` | Project directory containing .mtix/ (overrides cwd) |  |
---

## plugin

**Usage:** `plugin`

Install mtix as a plugin for AI coding agents

Install skill files, reference checklists, and MCP configuration
for IDE-specific AI agent integrations.

Supported targets: claude-code (default), cursor, windsurf.

### Subcommands

- `install` — Install skill files and MCP configuration
---

## install

**Usage:** `install`

Install skill files and MCP configuration

Install 5 skill files and 4 compliance reference checklists for the
target IDE's AI agent. Skills include safety-critical operating procedures
as baseline (not optional) — context chain traversal, independent verification,
traceability, and anomaly reporting.

For Claude Code: writes to .claude/skills/ (or ~/.claude/skills/ with --global).

### Flags

| Flag | Short | Description | Default |
|------|-------|-------------|---------|
| `--global` |  | Install to global skill directory (~/.claude/skills/) | false |
| `--target` |  | Target IDE: claude-code, cursor, windsurf | claude-code |
---

## rerun

**Usage:** `rerun <id>`

Invalidate and reprocess descendants of a node

Rerun invalidates descendants with configurable strategies:
  --strategy=all        Reset all descendants to open
  --strategy=open_only  Reset only non-done descendants
  --strategy=delete     Soft-delete all descendants
  --strategy=review     Set descendants to invalidated for review

### Flags

| Flag | Short | Description | Default |
|------|-------|-------------|---------|
| `--reason` |  | Reason for the rerun | rerun via CLI |
| `--strategy` |  | Rerun strategy (all, open_only, delete, review) | all |
---

## sync

**Usage:** `sync`

Check or fix sync between SQLite and tasks.json

Compare the SQLite database with .mtix/tasks.json and report any drift.
Use --fix to re-export the database to tasks.json, resolving any discrepancies.

### Flags

| Flag | Short | Description | Default |
|------|-------|-------------|---------|
| `--fix` |  | Re-export database to tasks.json to resolve drift | false |
---
